rationale: The page provides detailed information on land registration systems in India, including the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) and its initiatives like ULPIN and NGDRS. It also covers the SVAMITVA scheme, its objectives, and implementation details.
cleanedText:
Types of Land Registration System
- Deeds Registration System: Deeds are registered in government record but registration does not itself guarantee title, ownership is proven by tracing chain of deeds.
- Title Registration (Torrens) System: Registration is conclusive proof of title, the government guarantees the register and ownership changes only with registration; the owner gets a certificate of title.
Measures taken to improve Land Record System in India
- DILRMP: Replace manual presumptive land-title system with a digital conclusive land titling system. It is a Central Sector Scheme.
- Initiatives under DILRMP
- Unique Land Parcel Identification Number (ULPIN) or Bhu-Aadhar: 14-digit Alpha–numeric unique ID for each land parcel based on Geo-coordinates to streamline real estate transactions.
- NGDRS or E-Registration: Empowers citizens through online entry of deed, online payment, online appointment, online admission, document search and certified copy generation.
- Linkage of e-Court with Land Records / Registration Data base: It makes authentic first-hand information available to Courts resulting in speedy disposal of cases and reduction in land disputes.
- Transliteration of Land Records: To overcome language barrier, transliterate Records of Rights in local language to any 22 languages listed in Schedule VIII of Constitution.
- Digitalise Land Record System of different States
- Dharani portal/Bhubharati portal Telangana: Modernize land administration by digitizing land records and streamlining transactions.
- Kaveri portal, Karnataka: Reform land registration.
- Banglarbhumi platform, West Bengal: Provide easy access to land and property records.
8.6. 5 YEARS OF SVAMITVA SCHEME
Why in the News?
The SVAMITVA (Survey of Villages and Mapping with Improvised Technology in Village Areas) Scheme has completed 5 years of its launch.
About SVAMITVA
- Launch: 2020
- Implementation: Ministry of Panchayati Raj in collaboration with State Revenue Departments, State Panchayati Raj Departments, and the Survey of India.
- Key Technology Partners: Survey of India and National Informatics Centre Services Inc (NICSI).
- Major Components: Continuously Operating Reference Stations (CORS) Network: Establishes ground control points for precise geo-referencing and land demarcation.
SVAMITVA objectives
- Local: Property Tax - Funds local governance and development.
- National: Survey Infrastructure - Creates GIS maps for government use.
- Financial Stability: Empowers citizens with property assets.
- Accurate Records: Reduces disputes, enables rural planning.
- GPDP Preparation: Improves spatial data for planning.
